Items

Victorian Era Literature (1832-1901)

Abraham, Abraham. A Catechism of Religious and Moral Instruction for Children of the Hebrew Faith. Liverpool, 1840.

---. Moral and Religious Tales for the Young of the Hebrew Faith, Adapted from the French of “Les Matinées du Samedi” of G. Ben Levi. Whittaker, 1846.

Abraham, Philip. The Autobiography of a Jewish Gentleman. London, 1860.

---. Autumn Gatherings, Being a Collection of Prose and Poetry. J. Wertheimer, 1866.

---. Curiosities of Judaism: Facts, Opinions, Anecdotes, and Remarks Relative to the Hebrew Nation. Wertheimer, Lea and Co., 1879.

Abrahams (Sussman), Abraham. Millel Avraham. London, 1882.

---. Zekhor le-Avraham. London, 1860.

Abrahams, Israel. Chapters on Jewish Literature. Jewish Publication Society of America, 1899.

---. Jewish Life in the Middle Ages. Jewish Publication Society of America, 1896.

---, editor. Jewish Quarterly Review. 1888–1908.

Abrahams, Israel, and Claude Montefiore. Aspects of Judaism. 2nd ed., Macmillan and Co., 1895.

Abrahams, Louis Barnett, editor. The Jewish Record. 1868–c.1871.

---. A Manual of Scripture History for use in Jewish Schools and Families. 2nd ed., Trubner, 1882.

---. Murby’s Chronological History of England. Thomas Murby, 1870.

Adler, Hermann. “Can Jews Be Patriots?” Littell’s Living Age, vol. 137, no. 1769, 11 May 1878, p. 374.

---. A Course of Sermons on the Biblical Passages Adduced by Christian Theologians in Support of the Dogmas of Their Faith, Preached in the Bayswater Synagogue. Trubner & Co., 1869.

---. The Cry of Our Board School Children: A Sermon Preached at the St. John’s Wood Synagogue, on Sabbath, December 1st, 1894.

---. Fidelity to the Dead: A Sermon Preached at the New Synagogue on February 9th, 5655–1895.

---. The Ideal Jewish Pastor: A Sermon, Preached at the Great Synagogue, on June 23rd, 5651–1891. Wertheimer, Lea and Co., 1891.

---. The Industrious Apprentice: A Sermon Preached at the Great Synagogue on Sabbath December 10th, 1887. Wertheimer, Lea and Co., 1887.

---. The Jews During the Victorian Era: A Sermon Preached at the North London Synagogue on Sabbath, June 26th, 5657–1897. Alfred J. Isaacs & Sons, 1897.

---. Judaism and War: A Sermon Preached at the North London Synagogue on Sabbath, November 4th, 5660–1899.

---. Our Provincial Brethren: A Sermon Preached at the North London Synagogue, on Sabbath, July 13th, 5661–1901.

---. The Sabbath and the Synagogue: A Sermon Preached at the North London Synagogue, on June 29th, 5649–1889.

Adler, Marcus Nathan. The Itinerary of Benjamin of Tudela: Critical Text, Translation and Commentary. Oxford University Press, 1907.

Adler, Michael. Elements of Hebrew Grammar: With Exercises. David Nutt, 1897.

---. Students’ Hebrew Grammar. David Nutt, 1900.

Adler, Nathan Marcus. The Bonds of Brotherhood; A Sermon, Delivered in the Synagogue Sha’ar Hashamayim of the Spanish and Portuguese Congregation in London. J. Wertheimer, 1849.

---. How Can the Blessings of the House of God be Attained? A Sermon Delivered on the Occasion of the Consecration of the Synagogue at Canterbury. Sherwood, Gilbert, and Piper, 1848.

---. The Jewish Faith; A Sermon Delivered in the Great Synagogue, Duke’s Place, Sabbath, 24 Shevat, 5608. J. Wertheimer, 1848.

---. The Morning and Evening Sacrifice; A Sermon Delivered in the Great Synagogue, Duke’s Place, on January 28, 5625. Wertheimer, 1865.

---. Netinah la-Ger (A Gift to the Proselyte). 1872. Romm, 1874.

---. The Second Days of the Festivals; A Sermon at the New Synagogue, Great St. Helen’s, on the Second Day of Passover, 5628. Trübner, 1868.

---. Sermon Delivered in the Great Synagogue, Duke’s Place, Wednesday, the 24th March, A.M., 5607. Sherwood, Gilbert, and Piper, 1847.

---. Sermon Delivered at the Great Synagogue, on the Occasion of His Installation into Office as Chief Rabbi Great Britain. Translated by Barnard Van Oven, Longman, Brown, Green and Longmans, 1845.

---. Solomon’s Judgment: A Picture of Israel; A Sermon Delivered at the Great Synagogue, on Shabbat Chanukah, 31st December, 5614. Wertheimer, 1854.

Aguilar, Emanuel. How to Learn the Pianoforte. Groombridge and Sons, 1883.

---. A Little Book About Learning the Pianoforte. Groombridge and Sons, 1866.

Aguilar, Emanuel Abraham, and Christina Rossetti. Goblin Market: Cantata, for Treble Voices. Hutchings & Romer, 1880.

Aguilar, Emanuel Abraham, and David Aaron de Sola. The Ancient Melodies of the Liturgy of the Spanish and Portuguese Jews. Wertheimer, 1857.

Aguilar, Grace. See Bibliography of Grace Aguilar.

Alexander, Samuel. Moral Order and Progress: An Analysis of Ethical Conceptions. Trubner, 1889.

Amschejewitz, Asher. Sefer Asher Ha-Midot. 1879.

---. Shem Olam. 1864.

Angel, Moses, editor. The Jewish Chronicle, 1841–1842.

Angel, Moses. The Law of Sinai and its Appointed Times. William Tegg, 1858.

Artom, Benjamin. Sermons Preached in Several Synagogues. Trubner, 1873.

Ascher, Benjamin, trans. The Book of Life (Sefer Hachayim) by Simon Frankfor. Meldola, Cahn, & Co., 1847.

---, trans. Mivhar ha-Peninim (A Choice of Pearls) by Solomon ben Gabirol, 1859.

Asher, Asher. “Jewish Rite of Circumcision,” 1873.

Ballin, Ada Sarah. From Cradle to School: A Book for Mothers. Constable, 1902.

Ballin, Ada Sarah, and F. L. Ballin. A Hebrew Grammar with Exercises Selected from the Bible. C. Kegan Paul & Co., 1881.

Ballin, Ada Sarah, ed. Nursery Cookery. F. L. Ballin, 1900.

---. The Science of Dress in Theory and Practice. Sampson Low, Marston, Searle, & Rivington, 1885.

Behrend, Henry. “Essay on the Post-Biblical History of the Jews,” 1850.

---. “A Defense of the Mosaic Regulations.”

Belisario, Miriam Mendes. A Hebrew and English Vocabulary from a Selection of the Daily Prayers. 1848. P. Vallentine, 1878.

---. Sabbath Evenings at Home. 2 vols., S. Joel, 1856.

Benisch, Abraham. Bishop Colenso’s Objections to the Historical Character of the Pentateuch and the Book of Joshua. The Jewish Chronicle, 1863.

---. Elementary Hebrew Grammar. 1852.

---. Judaism Surveyed; Being a Sketch of the Rise and Development of Judaism, from Moses to our Days, in a Series of Five Lectures. Longmans, Green & Co., 1874.

---. Judaïsme et Christianisme. Translated by Alexandre Weill, E. Dentu Libraire-Éditeur, 1878.

---. The Principal Charges of Dr. M’Caul’s Old Paths, as Stated by Mr. Newdegate in the House of Commons, Considered & Answered. The Jewish Chronicle, 1858.

---. Two Lectures on the Life and Writings of Maimonides. Wertheim, 1847.

---, editor. Scripture History, Simply Arranged for the Use of Jewish Children. Sophia Joel, 1853.

---, trans. Jewish School and Family Bible. Vols. 1–4, James Darling and Longman & Co., 1851–1861.

---, trans. The Pentateuch, and the Haftaroth. Vols. 1–5, 2nd ed., J. Lehrberger & Comp., 1864.

---, trans. Travels of Rabbi Petachia of Ratisbon. By Petachia of Ratisbon, Messrs. Trubner & Co., 1856.

Bennett, Solomon Yom Tov. Critical Remarks on the Authorized Versions of the Old Testament. London, 1834.

---. A Theological and Critical Treatise on the Primogeniture and Integrity of the Holy Language. Richard Taylor, 1835.

Bentwich, Herbert. “The Progress of Zionism.” The Fortnightly Review, Dec. 1898, pp. 928–943.

Bergson, Michael Gabriel. A Collection of Sacred Jewish Hymns and Prayers, in Hebrew, English and German, with Solos and Choruses, and an Accompaniment for Piano or Harmonium. Edited by Marcus Hast, 1874.

Blind, Mathilde. Birds of Passage: Songs of the Orient and Occident. Chatto & Windus, 1895.

---. “Mary Wollstonecraft.” New Quarterly Magazine, vol. 10, July 1878, pp. 390–412.

---, trans. The Old Faith and the New: A Confession. By David Friedrich Strauss, Asher & Co., 1873.

---. The Poetical Works of Mathilde Blind. Edited by Arthur Symons, with a memoir by Richard Garnett, T. Fisher Unwin, 1900.

---. A Selection from the Poems of Mathilde Blind. Edited by Arthur Symons, T. Fisher Unwin, 1897.

---. “Shelley.” Review of The Poetical Works of Percy Bysshe Shelley: A Revised Text, with Notes and a Memoir, edited by William Michael Rossetti. Westminster Review, vol. 38, July 1870, pp. 75–97.

Boas, Frederick Samuel. Shakespeare and His Predecessors. 1896. John Murray, 1910.

Bresslau, Marcus, ed. The Hebrew Review. Vol. 1, nos. 1–48, 1860.

---. Kitzur Dikduk Lashon Ivri (A Compendious Hebrew Grammar). John Weale, 1855.

Browning, Robert. “Ben Karshook’s Wisdom.” The Keepsake, 1856, p. 16.

---. “Holy-Cross Day.” Men and Women. Vol. 2, Chapman and Hall, 1855, pp. 158–166.

---. “Jochanan Hakkadosh.” Jocoseria. Smith, Elder & Co., 1883, pp. 71–132.

---. “Rabbi Ben Ezra.” Dramatis Personae. Chapman & Hall, 1864, pp. 77–87.

---. “Saul.” Men and Women. Vol. 2, Chapman and Hall, 1855, pp. 111–146.

---. “Solomon and Balkis.” Jocoseria. Smith, Elder & Co., 1883, pp. 25–32.

Chaikin, Avigdor. The Celebrities of the Jews. Pawson and Brailsford, 1899.

Chotzner, Joseph. Humour and Irony of the Hebrew Bible. London, 1883.

---. Modern Judaism. London, 1876.

---. Zikhronot. 1885.

Cohen, Alfred Louis. Prayers for Jewish Family Use. Wertheimer, Lea and Co., 1890.

Davids, Arthur Lumley. Lecture on the Philosophy of the Jews: Delivered at the London Tavern, to the Society for the Cultivation of Hebrew Literature, December 23, 1830. Parbury, Allen, & Co.; J. & A. Arch; John Taylor; W. Mason, 1833.

Disraeli, Benjamin. See Bibliography of Benjamin Disraeli.

Disraeli, Benjamin. “On the Life and Writings of Mr. D’Israeli.” Curiosities of Literature, by Isaac D’Israeli, 14th ed., vol. 1, Edward Moxon, 1849, pp. vii–xxxvii.

D’Israeli, Isaac. Curiosities of Literature: With a View of the Life and Writings of the Author by His Son. 14th ed., 3 vols., Edward Moxon, 1849.

---. The Genius of Judaism. Edward Moxon, 1833.

Edelmann, Zevi Hirsch. Derekh Tovim: The Path of Good Men. London, 1852.

Edelmann, Zevi Hirsch, and Leopold Dukes, editors. 1850. Treasures of Oxford: Containing Poetical Compositions by the Ancient Jewish Authors in Spain. Translated by Marcus Hyman Bresslau, R. Groombridge and Sons, 1851.

Edrehi, Moses ben Isaac. 1836. An Historical Account of the Ten Tribes, Settled Beyond the River Sambatyon in the East. Philadelphia, 1853.

Ezekiel, Solomon. A Lecture on the Hebrew Festivals, Delivered at the Penzance Literary Institute. F. T. Vibert, 1847.

Faudel, Henry. A Few Words on the Jewish Disabilities: Addressed to Sir Robert Harry Inglis, Bart., M.P. James Ridgway, 1848.

---. Suggestions to the Jews: For Improvement in Reference to Their Charities, Education, and General Government. John Wertheimer and Co., 1844.

Fernandes, Benjamin Dias. A Series of Letters on the Evidences of Christianity. Jewish Publication Society of America, 1853.

Mocatta, Isaac Lindo. The Jewish Armoury. Brighton, 1877.

---. Moral Biblical Gleanings and Practical Teachings, Illustrated by Biographical Sketches Drawn from the Sacred Volume. Trübner & Co., 1872.

Newman, Selig. The Challenge Accepted: A Dialogue Between a Jew and a Christian. Isaacs & Solomons, 1850.

---. Emendations of the English Version of the Old Testament. B. Wertheim, 1839.

---. Sefer ha-Shorashim [Book of Roots], A Hebrew and English Lexicon. Longman, Reese, Orme, Brown, and Green, 1834.

---, Sefer Milim [Book of Words], An English and Hebrew Lexicon. Longman, Rees, Orme, Brown, and Green, 1832.

 

Victorian Jewish Writers Project

Welcome to the Victorian Jewish Writers Project, a digital collection focusing on Anglo-Jewish writing during the long nineteenth century. The site is a work-in-progress, so please contact katzir [at] vjwp [dot] org if you need one-on-one assistance.
